revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天之御影神]]



{{Distinguish|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}{{Template:Infobox deity
| 名 = 天之御影神 
| 世代名 =
| 先代= 
| 次代= 
| 神祇 = {{ill|Heavenly Deities|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}
| 全名 =
| 別名 = 明立天御影命、天御影命
| 神階 = 
| 神格 = 
| 陵所 = 
| father = {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|Amatsuhikone|qid=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}}
| 母 =
| 親 = 
| 兄弟姉妹 =
| 配偶者 =
| children = {{ill|Okitsunagamizu-no-Irehime|ja|息長水依媛|lt=Okitsunagamizu-no-Irehime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsunagamizu-no-Irehime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（wife of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}})
| 宮 = 
| temple = {{ill|Mikami Shrine|en|Mikami Shrine|qid=Q11488762|lt=Mikami Shrine|1=Mikami Shrine}}
}}
'''Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami''' (天之御影神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|qid=Q524158|lt=kami|1=Kami}} featured in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. This deity is also referred to as '''Akateru-Ame-no-Mikage-no-Mikoto''' (明立天御影命) or simply '''Ame-no-Mikage-no-Mikoto''' (天御影命).
== Overview ==
Among the deities who accompanied {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} during his descent, Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami is one. According to sources such as the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Kujiki|1=Kujiki}} and the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, this deity's daughter, Okitsunagamizu-no-Irehime (息長水依媛), became the consort of Hikoza no Mikoto (彦坐王). Their 11th-generation descendant is Yamashiro-no-Neko (山代根子), with later descendants including the {{ill|Mikami clan|ja|三上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|凡河内国造|lt=Onokuchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshikōchi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|lt=Nukata clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukata clan|qq=}}, and the Yamano clan (山直).<ref>{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kujihonki|1=Kujiki}}</ref><ref>{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}</ref>
At {{ill|Mikami Shrine|en|Mikami Shrine|qid=Q11488762|lt=Mikami Shrine|1=Mikami Shrine}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, this deity is venerated as the ancestral deity of the Mikami clan (三上氏) and serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} of the {{ill|Yasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Anokuchi Province|ja|安国造|lt=Anokuchi no Kuni|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anokuchi Province|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Kenji Kawaguchi|ja|川口謙二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji Kawaguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. "Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods" ({{ill|Tokyo Bijutsu|ja|東京美術|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bijutsu|qq=}}, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
according to traditions of the {{ill|Okinaga Clan|ja|息長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Clan|qq=}} {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} may be the same god.
